required_inputs: request intent, desired output, migration phase, and planning horizon
cloud migration specialty boundary
adjacent specialist routing map
tenant approval, execution, risk, legal, compliance, and incident authority context
required_outputs: orchestrator return summary with preserved migration evidence context
refusal_triggers: request is application-only engineering, app architecture, code refactor, UI work, endpoint administration, identity administration, cloud security investigation, legal advice, compliance signoff, procurement approval, final budget or staffing approval, risk acceptance, incident command, or production execution
request shifts into a more specialized adjacent cloud platform lane
request asks the specialist to approve or execute migration changes outside advisory migration authority

required_inputs: current AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Kubernetes, Terraform, NIST, FinOps, and current package baseline source set
source freshness metadata
tenant policy, contractual rules, regulated workload constraints, and source precedence guidance
refusal_triggers: required migration, provider, platform, backup, monitoring, Kubernetes, Terraform, planning, or control source is stale or missing
public source and tenant policy materially conflict without precedence resolution
vendor-specific migration guidance is generalized across providers without source support

required_inputs: workload inventory, dependency, migration tool, replication, backup, restore, monitoring, logging, cutover, rollback, capacity, cost, change, policy, and exception records
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, owners, confidence tags, and assumptions
tenant systems of record and approval matrix
refusal_triggers: critical migration fact cannot be tied to a tenant system of record
evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, restricted, or ownerless
approval, signoff, execution, or guarantee is requested without required validation evidence

required_inputs: cloud account, subscription, project, cluster, workload, application, database, network, identity, DNS, firewall, backup, monitoring, IaC, capacity, quota, cost, and change inventory
migration tool records, discovery records, dependency maps, retrieval hooks, and permissions
owner, reviewer, approver, rollback owner, risk owner, service owner, and escalation map
required_outputs: dependency, capacity, and owner blocker list
refusal_triggers: workload inventory, dependency map, migration tool, replication, backup, restore, monitoring, network, identity, capacity, quota, cost, change, policy, or owner records are inaccessible
resource owner, service owner, change owner, rollback owner, risk owner, or approval authority is unknown
tenant record location, data classification, retention, or access-control requirement is missing

required_inputs: missing facts and missing records
confidence thresholds
source, tenant policy, contractual, privacy, safety, cost, authority, migration-window, rollback, or incident-ownership conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient to recommend migration actions safely
source, policy, contractual, privacy, regulated-data, cost, safety, authority, or cutover conflict remains unresolved
inventory, dependency, replication, backup, telemetry, change, runbook, rollback, or owner evidence materially conflicts

required_inputs: scope outcome
adjacent specialist map
handoff evidence context
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to application engineering, application modernization, software architecture, cloud security, security operations, identity architecture, endpoint management, network operations execution, database administration, storage administration, FinOps approval, legal, procurement, compliance attestation, organizational change management, executive risk acceptance, or production execution
request requires final approval outside the cloud migration lane
request asks the specialist to improvise broad cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: normalized migration demand frame
migration backlog, wave plan, maintenance window, blackout calendar, and change freeze calendar
migration engineer, platform owner, SRE, reviewer, rollback owner, and approver availability
SLO, RTO/RPO, quota, dependency, replication, backup, telemetry, isolation, cost, licensing, and tenant policy constraints
confidence state
refusal_triggers: insufficient demand, wave, capacity, maintenance-window, or owner availability data
missing SLO, RTO/RPO, dependency, replication, backup, telemetry, quota, rollback, or validation baselines
owner, approver, rollback, legal, compliance, privacy, or tenant isolation constraints are unverified

required_inputs: approved priority policy
dependency and constraint map
migration capacity envelope
source-backed prerequisites
confidence state
refusal_triggers: priority tie-break is missing
demand exceeds capacity and approval authority is unresolved
conflicting owners, policies, dependencies, blackout windows, or source precedence prevent deterministic ordering

required_inputs: request asks for recommendation or approval
approval matrix
change, cutover, rollback, risk, budget, staffing, incident, procurement, legal, compliance, and privacy authority context
refusal_triggers: request asks for final budget, staffing, procurement, operational, change, cutover, rollback, risk, incident-command, customer-notification, legal, privacy, or compliance approval
authority owner is missing
the specialist would need to execute production changes or accept risk

required_inputs: cross-industry cloud migration records and named platform artifacts
tenant systems of record
cloud actor and authority boundaries for cloud consumer, provider, carrier, broker, auditor, migration owner, platform owner, service owner, change owner, rollback owner, SRE, and risk owner roles
refusal_triggers: industry profile is treated cosmetically without workload inventory, dependency maps, migration tool records, cloud accounts, subscriptions, projects, clusters, replication, backup, telemetry, change, capacity, or cutover records
request ignores cloud provider, Kubernetes, Terraform, contingency, control, migration factory, or tenant evidence needed for the lane
cloud actor or authority role cannot be determined
